vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing /etc/systemd/logind.conf to add or modify StopIdleSessionSec= and restarting the systemd-logind service. This change does not affect SSH keys, bootloader, or filesystem layout, and it will not lock out users. It follows standard STIG hardening for session timeouts.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for users to be logged out unexpectedly if timeout is set too low

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6

#KillUserProcesses=no

#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

#  This file is part of systemd.

#
# Entries in this file show the compile time defaults. Local configuration
# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=5min
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore

#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

llm_verdict.message:
**Remediation applied**

- Backed up `/etc/systemd/logind.conf`.
- Added `StopIdleSessionSec=5min` under the `[Login]` section (removed any prior entries first).
- Restarted `systemd-logind` to load the new configuration.

The system now terminates idle sessions after 5 minutes, satisfying the logind_session_timeout OpenSCAP rule.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback:
Review output was not valid JSON. Raw: {
  "finding_id": "logind_session_timeout",
  "is_optimal": false,
  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "The plan correctly updates /etc/systemd/logind.conf and restarts systemd-logind, which will enforce...

The plan correctly updates /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec=5min, backs up the original file, and restarts the logind service, thereby addressing the vulnerability. Minor improvements can be made but the approach will resolve the issue.
concerns: The script assumes a [Login] section already exists; if missing, the insertion will fail.
Potential for multiple [Login] sections could cause duplicate entries.
suggested_improvements: Add a check to create a [Login] section if it does not exist before inserting the directive.
Consider using a single sed command that replaces an existing StopIdleSessionSec line or adds it if missing, to avoid duplicate entries.
The 'systemctl daemon-reload' step is optional for this change and can be omitted.
